nodes.title: ATLAS QUICKSTART — GHOST TOWN / CAMERA-AWARE RELIEF — PROJECT / GRAY REVIEW

widgets_values:
THE MARKETING MOVE:
1. Open 🎥 Camera Path (on the Output Desk)
2. Click a move (⟳ Orbit R / ⭢ Dolly In)
3. Set the 🔭 lens to taste
4. ⏺ Bake Proxy Path → the graph re-queues and
   the sequence/video/USD nodes below fill in.

path_frames is a 1-frame placeholder until ⏺ Bake runs — the JPEG
saver and MP4 write a single black frame on the first queue, then the
real 100-frame 24fps move after baking. The USD export ships BYPASSED
(it errors pre-bake by design): un-bypass after ⏺ Bake.

nodes.title: Reproject from the EXACT extraction pose
PATCH REVIEW — holes filled from the exact pose

widgets_values:
THE PHOTOSHOP ROUND TRIP (gated):
This whole group is PAUSED until you
click 📐 Extract Angle on the Output Desk
(orbit to the hole first, then ⬛ Render
Proxy Passes, then 📐).

1. Re-queue → the extract node writes
   patch.png + matte + plate_full +
   sidecar to atlas_exports/.../angle_patch
2. Edit patch.png in Photoshop.
   DO NOT resize the canvas.
3. Save into ComfyUI/input, point the
   bypassed LoadImage at it, un-bypass.
4. Re-queue → the edit is pasted back
   into the full frame and reprojected
   from the EXACT extraction camera
   (patch_exact, never re-estimated).

The matte is the holes themselves:
NOT(viewport mask pass).

nodes.title: ⬅ CLEANPLATE INJECTION PORT — un-bypass after painting
depth of the ORIGINAL (the car's geometry)

widgets_values:
FG OCCLUDER LAYER:
the car + fallen sign keep their
ORIGINAL pixels on their OWN
depth geometry, matte-cut by a
deterministic SegFormer mask.

The base scene projects the
CLEANPLATE — so orbits reveal
painted street behind them,
not inpaint smear.

Front layer keeps a CLEAN cut
(edge_extend 0) per the seam
doctrine.

🧽 THE CLEANPLATE DOCTRINE (do this FIRST, before queueing):
1. Open the plate in Photoshop and paint OUT the foreground
   occluders (here: the car and the fallen sign). Content-aware
   fill / clone at source quality — this is the classic DMP prep.
2. Save it into ComfyUI/input, point the 🧽 LoadImage at it,
   un-bypass the INJECTION PORT.
3. Queue. The cleanplate becomes the projection plate for the
   BASE scene (solve, depth, mesh, exports); the original pixels
   ride the FG OCCLUDER layer (group 0b) on their own geometry.

Bypassed (as shipped) the port forwards the original untouched,
so the workflow runs out-of-box. 📐 Extract Angle (group 4) is
then only for RESIDUAL fixes: small stretching on the cleanplate
mesh at grazing angles — not for painting out whole objects.

nodes.title: 🧽 CLEANPLATE STACK — up to 4 painted plates + alphas (optional)

widgets_values:
🧽 FULL SEPARATION (optional, artist's choice):
paint the plate into up to FOUR strata in Photoshop —
e.g. sky / mountains / buildings / dirt road — each a
full-frame plate + alpha. Save each as PNG-with-alpha
and wire ONE LoadImage per slot:
   IMAGE -> plate_N,  MASK -> matte_N
then turn mattes_are_transparency ON (LoadImage's MASK
marks transparent pixels).

Slot 1 = FARTHEST stratum, slot 4 = nearest. Priorities
are farthest-highest automatically, behind slots get the
edge smear, the nearest keeps a clean cut (seam doctrine
baked in). Unwired slots are skipped; with none wired the
solve passes through untouched — as shipped.

nodes.title: 🧩 SAM3 — car + sign matte (simple noun phrases + 'and')

widgets_values:
🎨 ARTIST MATTE OVERRIDE (parked):
The painted cleanplate's own region IS the fg
matte — one Photoshop session gives you both.
Save the matte as a PNG with alpha, point this
LoadImage at it, un-bypass both nodes, then wire
the InvertMask output into the FG OCCLUDER
layer's layer_matte (replacing the SAM3 chain).

SAM3 with the shadow-inclusive prompt is the
automatic default; the artist matte is the
hero-shot override.

groups.title: 0 · PLATE IN + 🧽 CLEANPLATE INJECTION PORT — paint occluders out in Photoshop FIRST
1 · FG OCCLUDER — original pixels, SAM3 matte (or the 🎨 artist override)
2 · 🧽 CLEANPLATE STACK — up to 4 painted strata (optional)
3 · OUTPUT DESK + VIEWPORT — author the move here
5 · 📐 ANGLE PATCH — residual cleanup only (gated until Extract Angle)

extra.workflow_name: ATLAS QUICKSTART — GHOST TOWN / CAMERA-AWARE RELIEF
extra.description: Start here: one plate, MoGe-2 metric depth, masked 1024 relief, and an optional camera move.
